stories.headline: US-Iran War: Strait of Hormuz closure, oil prices spike, and coalition efforts

articles.title: Trump threatens NATO allies over Strait of Hormuz help
articles.description: With the Iran war entering a third week and the price of oil reaching nearly $105 a barrel on Monday, President Trump again urged NATO countries and China to help the U.S. secure the vital Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Morning news brief
articles.description: The war with Iran enters its third week, as Trump and his top aides refocus their messaging on "winning" to regain faltering support, in the Senate, Republican lawmakers take up the SAVE Act this week.

articles.title: Iran war keeps Strait of Hormuz paralyzed and oil prices over $100
articles.description: Iran war could escalate further as Trump threatens to hit key oil infrastructure if Tehran doesn't drop its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Iran's foreign minister rejects talk of dialogue with U.S. as strikes continue
articles.description: The war, which shows no signs of ending soon, has disrupted oil exports from the region, driven fuel prices far higher worldwide and upended global air travel.

articles.title: The Iran war is making it harder for Fed officials to cut interest rates
articles.description: Some economists think the Fed, facing inflationary pressures from rising energy prices, may not cut interest rates at all this year.

articles.title: Iranian foreign minister says "we don't see any reason" to talk with U.S.
articles.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said that "we don't see any reason why we should talk with Americans" as President Trump has claimed Iran is seeking a deal to end the war between the U.S. and Iran.

articles.title: Intrigue, Power Plays and Rivalries: Inside the Rise of Mojtaba Khamenei
articles.description: The weeklong fight over Iran’s next leader pitted the Revolutionary Guards against moderates. The generals won, but only over spirited resistance.

articles.title: Oil Tops $106 a Barrel as Worries Persist About Global Supplies
articles.description: As the broader war in the Middle East begins its third week, worries over the fallout of crippled energy supplies continue to roil markets.

articles.title: 16 Reader Questions on the War in Iran and Our Reporting, Answered
articles.description: Do the United States and Israel share similar goals? What kind of leadership will Iran have next? How does The Times report on wartime deaths?

articles.title: Trump Asks for Help With Strait of Hormuz, and Israeli Forces Kill Family in West Bank
articles.description: Plus, highlights from the Oscars.

articles.title: Trump calls for other nations to help reopen Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: President Trump’s call for help to reopen the Strait of Hormuz has so far gone unanswered. On Truth Social, the president listed China, France, Japan, South Korea, the U.K. as countries he hopes would help. It comes as Trump tells NBC News that Iran is ready to negotiate a ceasefire. NBC News’ Raf Sanchez has the latest.

articles.title: Chris Wright addresses concerns over surging energy prices

articles.title: Iran war casts shadow over Middle East AI investments
articles.description: The U.S. war with Iran is spilling out across the Middle East, putting AI infrastructure at risk and increasingly raising questions about large-scale investments by American tech firms in the region. Industry leaders have committed billions of dollars to delivering chips and building data centers in wealthy Gulf nations like Saudi Arabia and the United…

articles.title: IDF says it’s planning for at least 3 more weeks of Iran operation
articles.description: Israel Defense Forces (IDF) on Sunday said it’s planning to continue strikes on Iran for at least three more weeks. “We have thousands of targets ahead,” IDF spokesperson Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin told CNN.  “We are ready, in coordination with our U.S. allies, with plans through at least the Jewish holiday of Passover, about three…

articles.title: Trump raises Democratic attacks by downplaying high fuel prices sparked by Iran war
articles.description: President Trump is downplaying concerns about rising gas and oil prices that have been badly exacerbated by the war in Iran. Both the president and members of his Cabinet have described rising prices as a “small price to pay” to achieve their goals in Iran. Trump also argued that rising prices are good for the…

articles.description: National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett on Sunday said the Trump administration has “got what we need” when it comes to funding for the conflict in Iran. “Well, tell me how much is this war going to cost? Because I know the Pentagon briefed it’s going to be about $11.3 billion. Do you need to…

articles.title: Senate Democrat: Congress ‘feckless’ in handing over war powers
articles.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) on Sunday said Congress has been “feckless” in allowing President Trump’s authority to expand and cited the war with Iran as an example of Congress failing to check the executive branch’s growing power. “Well, listen, I’m going to be one of those Democrats that say I think both parties have been…

articles.title: Pence on Iran conflict: ‘It’s about finishing a war that’s nearly 50 years in the making’
articles.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence on Sunday said President Trump’s strikes on Iran are “finishing” a decades-long war with leaders in Tehran. “One administration after another has slashed at the tentacles of Iran that have taken violence to American forces, to American citizens, to our allies in the region, most especially Israel, and making that…

articles.title: Warner on deadly Iranian girls’ school strike: ‘I want a thorough investigation’
articles.description: Sen. Mark Warner (D-Va.) on Sunday discussed a recent strike on an Iranian girls’ school, saying he desires “a thorough investigation” into the matter. “We’ve only got preliminary assessments, and I want a thorough investigation. But what I don’t want to do is jump to the conclusion, whether it was [U.S. Central Command (Centcom)] or…

articles.title: Trump says he asked ‘about seven’ countries to help defend Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: President Donald Trump revealed on Sunday night that he has talked to “about seven” countries regarding the Strait of Hormuz, as he looks to form a coalition to reopen and defend the key waterway from Iranian attacks. “I’m demanding that these countries come in and protect their own territory, because it is their own territory,” […]

articles.title: Trump talks with Starmer as he tries to lure countries to help reopen Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: President Donald Trump spoke with U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer by phone on Sunday, a conversation that touched on Iran‘s chokehold of the Strait of Hormuz. According to a statement from Starmer’s office, the two discussed the Iran war and the “importance of reopening the Strait of Hormuz to end the disruption to global shipping.” […]

articles.title: Oil and gas prices are soaring. Some countries are ready with solar panels and EVs
articles.description: As an energy crisis grows, some countries are more prepared because of renewable energy and electric vehicles. Pakistan reduced its reliance on imported natural gas because of the growth of solar.

analysis.summary: The US-Iran war has entered its third week with the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed to commercial shipping, pushing oil prices above $100 per barrel. President Trump has called on approximately seven countries to join a coalition to reopen the strait, while Iran's foreign minister has rejected any dialogue with the United States. The conflict has triggered global economic disruptions including rising fuel prices, disrupted air travel, and pharmaceutical supply chain concerns.
agreements: The US-Iran war is entering its third week (approximately day 16-17)
The Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed to commercial shipping
Oil prices have surpassed $100 per barrel
President Trump is calling on other nations to help secure and reopen the Strait of Hormuz
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi rejected dialogue with the United States
The U.S. struck military targets on Iran's Kharg Island
Iran has been attacking Gulf targets including in the UAE and Dubai
Rising energy prices are causing significant global economic disruption
A U.S. KC-135 refueling plane crashed in Iraq
Trump claims Iran is ready to negotiate, which Iran denies

disagreements.topic: Framing and naming of the conflict

positions.position: Consistently frames it as 'US-Israel war on Iran,' emphasizing joint US-Israeli aggression

positions.source: Most US sources (NPR, CBS, NBC, etc.)
positions.position: Frame it as 'Iran war' or 'war with Iran,' centering Iran as the subject rather than the object of military action

disagreements.topic: Allied response to Trump's coalition request

positions.position: Reports Japan and Australia explicitly said they have no plans to send ships
Reports Trump's call has 'so far gone unanswered'
Headlines that Trump says other nations are 'joining' US patrols in the Strait of Hormuz
Reports UK PM Starmer discussed the 'importance of reopening the Strait of Hormuz' with Trump

disagreements.topic: Expected duration of the war

positions.position: Energy Secretary Chris Wright says the war could be over in '4 to 6 weeks'
Reports IDF says it's planning for at least 3 more weeks of operations, through Passover
Reports the war 'shows no signs of ending soon'

disagreements.topic: Emphasis on economic vs. military dimensions

positions.position: Focus heavily on financial market impacts, Fed interest rate implications, and possibility of a rate hike
Emphasize humanitarian and civilian impacts including Gulf civilian targets, hospital scenes, and disrupted daily life
Emphasize military progress and that the US is 'steadily destroying' Iran's war capabilities

disagreements.topic: Whether the US is achieving its military objectives

positions.position: Cites Institute for the Study of War assessment that progress has been 'relatively positive' with significant damage to Iran's military capabilities
Reports ambassador Waltz claims US delivered 'dominant victory' over Iran
Analysis frames Trump as having been 'knocked back on his political heels' two weeks into the war
Acknowledges Iran has found leverage through the Strait of Hormuz despite military losses

uniqueAngles.angle: Reported on the internal power struggle for Iran's next supreme leader, detailing how Mojtaba Khamenei was selected after a weeklong fight between the Revolutionary Guards and moderates
Reported that National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett referenced a Pentagon briefing estimating the war cost at approximately $11.3 billion
Reported that the Iran war is putting Middle East AI data center investments by American tech firms at risk

EU's Kallas proposed a Black Sea model to unblock the Strait of Hormuz

Middle East war has disrupted pharmaceutical air routes, risking cancer drug supplies
Pope Leo XIV called for a ceasefire during his noon Angelus prayer from the Vatican
Trump spoke by phone with UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer specifically discussing the Strait of Hormuz
FBI issued an alert warning of an Iran drone threat in California
Reports that Iran's injured leader was flown to Moscow
China continues importing Iranian oil through a 'backdoor' route bypassing the Strait of Hormuz
The U.S. urged Americans to leave Iraq immediately
Planet Labs delayed publication of satellite imagery during the war, creating a 'picture blackout'
At least one person was killed in Abu Dhabi and a fire broke out in Fujairah from Iranian attacks; Dubai temporarily suspended flights after a drone hit a fuel tank near the airport
Energy Secretary Chris Wright described the Strait of Hormuz disruption as the largest oil supply disruption in history
Some countries like Pakistan are better weathering the energy crisis due to prior investments in solar energy and reduced reliance on imported natural gas
Turkey's Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan discussed diplomatic efforts in an exclusive interview as the regional war rages
Ted Cruz and Tucker Carlson reignited a public feud over the Iran war, with Cruz calling Carlson a 'demagogue' spreading antisemitism
Tehran claimed the US attacked it from UAE territory as the war entered its third week
Sen. Cory Booker called Congress 'feckless' for failing to check executive war powers in the Iran conflict

During his opening monologue, the former talk show host mostly avoided political topics, instead focusing his humor on the entertainment industry, most notably Netflix CEO Ted Sarandos, actor Leonardo DiCaprio, and actor Timothee Chalamet, in the […]

analysis.summary: At the 98th Academy Awards hosted by Conan O'Brien, Paul Thomas Anderson's political thriller 'One Battle After Another' won Best Picture and Best Director among six total awards. Michael B. Jordan won Best Actor for 'Sinners' and Jessie Buckley won Best Actress for 'Hamnet,' while the ceremony also featured the debut of a new Best Casting award and several historic firsts.
agreements: 'One Battle After Another' won Best Picture
Paul Thomas Anderson won Best Director
Michael B. Jordan won Best Actor for 'Sinners'
Jessie Buckley won Best Actress for 'Hamnet'
The ceremony was the 98th Academy Awards
Conan O'Brien hosted the ceremony
A new Best Casting award was introduced this year

stories.headline: Severe winter storms bring blizzard, tornado warnings and extreme weather across the US

articles.title: Severe storms pummel parts of U.S. with snow, high winds, and risk of tornados
articles.description: A broad and erratic patchwork of severe weather rumbled across much of the U.S. on Sunday, dumping heavy snow in the Upper Midwest while damaging high winds swept across the Plains.

articles.title: WATCH:  Powerful cross-country storm to slam Midwest and barrel toward the East Coast
articles.description: WABC's Meteorologist Dani Beckstrom updates on March storm sweeping across the country, leaving millions of Americans on alert of severe weather.

articles.title: WATCH:  Trouble in the skies causes travel woes for millions
articles.description: Airports are being rocked by weather delays, cancellations and TSA staffing issues leaving officials to advise travelers to add "at least three hours before departure" to navigate.

articles.title: Weather threats bring blizzard conditions, early heat wave to parts of U.S.
articles.description: More than 11.5 million people are under blizzard warnings, another 4.3 million are under winter storm warnings, and about 20.6 million are under an extreme heat watch, according to forecasters.

articles.title: Heavy Snow Warning As 12 Inches Set to Strike—‘Life-Threatening’
articles.description: Blizzard and whiteout conditions, could make travel in certain areas "treacherous and potentially life-threatening.”

articles.title: Tornado Watch Alerts Issued for Five States
articles.description: The National Weather Service has warned Southern states that weather conditions may lead to the formation of storms and tornadoes.

articles.title: US forecasts blizzard, polar vortex, heat dome and atmospheric river all at once - AP News
articles.description: US forecasts blizzard, polar vortex, heat dome and atmospheric river all at once  AP News

articles.title: Wicked winds, severe storms to wreak havoc in US, forcing school closures  and even Congress to cancel votes
articles.description: Severe storms and damaging winds are threatening to bring the Washington, DC, area to its knees Monday afternoon -- with school closures planned and Congress nixing votes.

articles.title: Snow and Wind Batter Parts of US, with Threat of Thunderstorms and Tornadoes Starting Later Sunday - Newsmax
articles.description: Snow and Wind Batter Parts of US, with Threat of Thunderstorms and Tornadoes Starting Later Sunday  Newsmax

articles.title: Extreme Weather Event to Spread Blizzard, Tornado Conditions Across Several States - theepochtimes.com
articles.description: Extreme Weather Event to Spread Blizzard, Tornado Conditions Across Several States  theepochtimes.com

analysis.summary: A massive, multi-faceted weather system swept across the United States in mid-March 2026, bringing simultaneous blizzard conditions to the Upper Midwest, tornado threats to Southern states, extreme heat out West, and damaging winds across the Plains and into the Washington, DC area. The storm disrupted travel, closed schools, and even forced Congress to cancel votes.
agreements: A broad severe weather system affected large portions of the United States simultaneously

Tornado threats were issued for southern states
High winds and damaging storms were a significant component of the weather event
The storm caused significant travel disruptions

disagreements.topic: Scope of simultaneous weather phenomena described

positions.position: Emphasizes four simultaneous phenomena: blizzard, polar vortex, heat dome, and atmospheric river
Focuses on blizzard conditions and an early heat wave

disagreements.topic: Primary framing of the story's impact

Frames the weather as a broad, erratic patchwork affecting multiple regions

uniqueAngles.angle: Provided specific population figures: more than 11.5 million under blizzard warnings, 4.3 million under winter storm warnings, and about 20.6 million under an extreme heat watch

Reported that tornado watch alerts were issued for five states and that up to 12 inches of snow was expected in some areas
Identified four simultaneous weather phenomena: blizzard, polar vortex, heat dome, and atmospheric river occurring at once
